note: Plinth bundled scatter-asset set — phase S4a curation output. See docs/SCATTER.md ("Bundled assets", "Scale anchor: 28-32 mm heroic") and docs/SCATTER-ASSETS.md ("Curation verdict", "Scan-quality pass result") in the stl-pack repo for the admission gate this set was built against. All pieces: CC0, single manifold shell, binary STL, <=15k tris, normalized Z-up/centered-XY/floored-Z0 in Blender 5.1.2.

assets.label: Hesperocyon skull

origin.source_variant: Low resolution 3D mesh, obj, scale in m (150k tris)

assets.size_reasoning: Small predator canid skull; picked 7mm (mid-low end of the 5-9mm skull band) to read as a modest beast skull beside 28mm scale.

assets.label: Pseudocynodictis skull

origin.source_variant: Low resolution 3D mesh, obj, scale in m (150k tris)

assets.size_reasoning: Smallest of the real-scan skulls (raw specimen 87mm vs Hesperocyon's 96mm); picked 6mm, the low end of the 5-9mm band.

assets.label: Leptophoca seal skull

origin.source_variant: Low resolution 3D mesh, obj, scale in m (150k tris)

assets.size_reasoning: Largest raw skull length in the set after the deer (171mm) and a heavier-boned predator silhouette; picked 8mm, upper-mid of the 5-9mm band.

assets.label: White-tailed deer skull

origin.collection: Smithsonian Open Access, NMNH Education & Outreach
origin.source_variant: Medium resolution 3D mesh, obj, scale in m (100k tris)

assets.size_reasoning: Longest raw skull in the set (266mm) and the only herbivore/game-animal read; picked 9mm, the top of the 5-9mm band, so it reads as the largest 'ordinary' skull short of the boomerang-head exception.

assets.label: Diplocaulus (boomerang-head) skull

origin.source_variant: Low resolution 3D mesh, obj, scale in m (150k tris)

assets.size_reasoning: Doc-flagged exception: the exotic flat 'boomerang' horn silhouette is unrecognizable at normal skull scale, so it earns the ~10-12mm allowance; picked 11mm.

assets.label: Deer mandible

origin.collection: Smithsonian Open Access, NMNH Education & Outreach
origin.source_variant: Medium resolution 3D mesh, obj, scale in m (100k tris)

assets.size_reasoning: Mid-large loose bone; picked 9mm (mid-high end of the 6-12mm bone band) to read clearly as a full jaw with both rami and tooth rows.

assets.label: Deer forelimb bone

origin.collection: Smithsonian Open Access, NMNH Education & Outreach
origin.source_variant: Medium resolution 3D mesh, obj, scale in m (100k tris)

assets.size_reasoning: Longest real specimen in the whole set (676mm raw); picked 12mm, the top of the 6-12mm bone band, to preserve its elongated long-bone read.

assets.label: Pilot whale mandible

origin.collection: Smithsonian Open Access, NMNH Vertebrate Zoology, Mammals Division
origin.source_variant: Medium resolution 3D mesh, obj, scale in m (100k tris)

assets.size_reasoning: Deliberate statement piece per the doc's whale-mandible anchor (~15-18mm); picked 16mm, well above the ordinary bone band.

assets.label: Mushroom
assets.size_reasoning: Doc anchor is 5-7mm tall; picked 6mm, the midpoint.

assets.label: Maple leaf

origin.author: see record_url (credited on the Printables page)

assets.size_reasoning: Leaf litter reads ~5mm at 28-32mm scale; XY normalized to 5mm, thickness set to 1.2mm (a real leaf is ~0.2mm but that prints as foil and vanishes into the 0.4mm stitch-sink), curl baked in.

assets.label: Apple leaf

origin.author: see record_url (credited on the Printables page)

assets.size_reasoning: Leaf litter reads ~5mm at 28-32mm scale; XY normalized to 5mm, thickness set to 1.2mm for printability, curl baked in.

assets.label: Cherry leaf

origin.author: see record_url (credited on the Printables page)

assets.size_reasoning: Leaf litter reads ~5mm at 28-32mm scale; XY normalized to 5mm, thickness set to 1.2mm for printability, curl baked in.

assets.label: Oak leaf

origin.author: see record_url (credited on the Printables page)
origin.source_variant: organic leaves set, oak (largest shell kept)

assets.size_reasoning: Leaf litter reads ~5mm at 28-32mm scale; XY normalized to 5mm, thickness set to 1.2mm for printability, curl baked in.

assets.label: Hazel leaf

origin.author: see record_url (credited on the Printables page)

assets.size_reasoning: Leaf litter reads ~5mm at 28-32mm scale; XY normalized to 5mm, thickness set to 1.2mm for printability, curl baked in.

assets.label: Broken forest branch
assets.size_reasoning: An 11mm broken branch fragment sized for visible bark and knot geometry at 28-32mm scale.

assets.label: Fallen forest log
assets.size_reasoning: A 16mm fallen-log statement piece with scanned cracks, broken ends, and decay in geometry.
